medical_history.chief_complaint: Enamel of her teeth was worm-eaten in appearance
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Severe polyhydramnios detected antenatally, poor feeding and failure to thrive at 2 months, diagnosed with impaired renal concentration at 1 year old, hypokalaemia and hypercalciuria at 18 months, diagnosed with amelogenesis imperfecta (AI) and bilateral nephrocalcinosis at 12 years old
medical_history.past_medical_history: Impaired renal concentration, hypokalaemia, hypercalciuria, amelogenesis imperfecta (AI), bilateral nephrocalcinosis
medical_history.family_history: Parents are first cousins, no siblings, no family history of diabetes mellitus or hearing loss

physical_examination.general_examination: Height 145 cm (-1.0SD), normal appearance and intelligence, normal secondary sex characteristics, normal blood pressure
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Extremely thin enamel, rough and extensively cracked surface of permanent teeth, bilateral nephrocalcinosis identified by ultrasonography

laboratory_examinations.findings: Normal renal function (serum creatinine level: 0.3 mg/dl), normal electrolyte levels (Na: 137 meq/l, K: 4.0 meq/l, Cl: 103 meq/l), normal blood gas findings (HCO3: 21.6 mmol/l, base excess (BE): -0.6) and normal serum osmolality (280 mOsm/kg), low urinary osmolality (150–250 mOsm/kg)

laboratory_examinations.procedure_name: Water deprivation test and vasopressin administration test
laboratory_examinations.findings: Initial serum antidiuretic hormone (ADH) level was high in spite of low urinary osmolality. Urine osmolality was less than that of serum after 4 hours of water deprivation and less than 300 mOsm/kg in spite of vasopressin administration. Total urine volume was 236 ml/kg/day.

Hypokalaemia (2.3 meq/l), hypercalciuria [urinary calcium creatinine ratio (Ca/Cr): 1.8 mg/mg; normal: 0.1–0.3], high plasma renin activity (14.4 ng/ml/h; normal: 0.3–2.9), high serum aldosterone levels (750 pg/ml; normal: 36–240)

Hypokalaemia (3.1–3.4 mEq/l), high plasma renin activity (11.0 ng/ml/h; normal: 0.3–2.9), high serum aldosterone level (512 pg/ml; normal: 36–240), normal calcium (9.8 mg/dl) and magnesium (2.1 mg/dl, normal 1.7–2.3) levels, normal renal function [blood urea nitrogen (BUN): 9 mg/dl; Cr: 0.51 mg/dl], normal serum osmolality (279 mOsm/kg), low urinary osmolality (136 mOsm/kg), hypercalciuria (urinary Ca/Cr: 1.1 mg/mg; normal: 0.1–0.3), normal urinary N-acetylglutamate (NAG) and a1-microblobulin excretion levels (urinary NAG: <3 U/l; urinary a1-microblobulin: 0.9 mg/l; normal: <8.3), no glucosuria or aminoaciduria, slightly high serum intact-parathyroid hormone (PTH) level (98.0 pg/ml; normal: 14–66), slightly high 1,25-(OH)2 Vit D level (103 pg/ml; normal: 20–70), normal protein induced by vitamin K antagonist II (PIVKAII) (PIVKAII: <1)

other_tests.findings: No mutation in any of the six genes (NKCC2, ROMK, CLC-Kb, Barttin, CaSR, AQP2)
